manipulated_prompt_obj.content:
Provide one single-word clue that relates to some of your team's words from the list below. You can choose to how many and to which words your clue relates to.
The goal is to have your partner guess all of your team's words based on the clues you give them, before the other team has uncovered all of theirs. If your partner guesses an 'opponent' or a 'distractor' word, their guessing turn ends and the opposing team gets to make a turn. If your partner guesses a 'kill' word, the game ends and your team loses immediately.
The more words your clue relates to, the quicker you can win the game, but the harder the associations may be to guess for your partner, so choose your clue word wisely. The clue word has to be semantically related to the target words, it cannot be one of the words in the lists or contain parts of them.

Always give your single-word clue and your comma-separated list of related target words in the following format and make your answers as short as possible, never include any other text than is required in this form:

CLUE: <WORD>
TARGETS: <TARGETS>

Your team words are: square, bermuda, amazon, cycle, snowman, tower, dragon, robot, platypus.
Your opponent's team words are: swing, dwarf, air, boom, doctor, file, disease, dress.
Distractor words are: hand, engine, apple, fork, thief, pie, embassy.
The kill words are: shark.

message.reasoning:
We need to generate a single word clue that relates to as many of our team's words as possible, without being too broad that it would also include opponent/distractor words or the kill word "shark". We must output in the format:

CLUE: <WORD>
TARGETS: <TARGETS>

Our team words: square, bermuda, amazon, cycle, snowman, tower, dragon, robot, platypus.

Opponent words: swing, dwarf, air, boom, doctor, file, disease, dress.

Distractor words: hand, engine, apple, fork, thief, pie, embassy.

Kill word: shark.

We need to give a clue that relates to some of the team words. The clue must be a single word, not any of the words in the lists, not contain parts of them (like "square" can't be "square").

Goal: Choose a clue that relates to many of our team words. The more the better, but must be careful not to be too ambiguous. The partner needs to guess the words, but we need to list the target words we intend the clue to refer to. The partner can guess any of our team words provided they are the target ones. If they guess an opponent or distractor word, the turn ends. If they guess the kill word, we lose.

We need to propose a clue that can be used to guess multiple of our team words. The clue must be semantically related to each of the target words.

Let's think about possible clues.

We have "square", "bermuda", "amazon", "cycle", "snowman", "tower", "dragon", "robot", "platypus".

We need a clue that relates to a subset.
